response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with impressive breadth, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His database background is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his only finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital, ~15 months) was in retail investment fintech, not fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets. His recent decade centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which does not satisfy the core requirements of this consulting engagement.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of requests/second) showing strong performance-engineering fundamentals
Excellent communication skills, evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and fluent English
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling experience
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL background is limited to MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; fintech exposure was brief and unrelated

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and communication skills
